points.headline: Nvidia becomes a PC chipmaker with the RTX Spark.
points.body: At Computex, Nvidia unveiled the RTX Spark, billed as "the most efficient PC chip ever built," its first complete Arm-based CPU-plus-GPU computing chip for laptops and mini-PCs shipping this fall. Microsoft is the launch partner, putting the chip at the heart of a new Surface Laptop Ultra and reviving a bet it once wrote off $900 million on. The move turns Nvidia into a direct rival to Intel, AMD, Apple, and Qualcomm in consumer computing.

points.headline: "The token becomes the unit of account" as AI gets expensive.
points.body: Commentators converged on one theme this week: AI pricing is reshaping the industry, with usage metered by tokens and Anthropic posting a standout revenue month off its consumption-based model. Simon Willison summed up May simply as "AI got expensive," and some users are now openly weighing whether to cancel subscriptions as costs climb.

points.headline: The compute buildout keeps scaling up.
points.body: SoftBank said it will invest up to €75 billion in French data centers to add 5 gigawatts of capacity, while Intel launched its first 2nm-class Xeon with 288 cores aimed squarely at AI agent workloads. With bills mounting, engineers are also fighting back: a Netflix engineer open-sourced "Project Headroom" to slash AI serving costs.

points.headline: Agentic AI's security holes are widening.
points.body: Arm open-sourced Metis, an agentic security framework that uses semantic reasoning to find software vulnerabilities and reportedly outperforms traditional SAST tools. At the same time, researchers showed a ChatGPT integration for Google Sheets could be coaxed into exfiltrating entire workbooks, underscoring how tool-connected agents expand the attack surface through prompt injection.

points.headline: Physical AI goes open with NVIDIA Cosmos 3.
points.body: Nvidia released Cosmos 3, described as the first open omni-model for physical AI reasoning and action, aimed at robots and embodied agents. The push lands alongside steady industrial momentum: MISUMI committed $1 billion to AI-driven digital manufacturing in the Americas, and open-source hardware continues to lower the barrier to building robots.

points.headline: AI coding reshapes how software gets built, and developers push back.
points.body: Posts on the speed of AI prototyping kept trending as coding agents like Codex find their own "workarounds" to get tasks done. But the developer mood is mixed: GitHub Copilot's new token-based billing drew loud complaints, and even AI boosters are questioning whether the always-on subscription is worth it.
